title: excoecaria magenjensis
intro: Excoecaria magenjensis, also known as the Poison Tree or Maganja poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Papua New Guinea and the Pacific Islands. The plant contains a toxic compound called phorbol, which has medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Excoecaria magenjensis is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, or ointments, although caution is advised due to its toxic compounds and potential side effects.
